drafts.title: Building in public: why I open-sourced my agent framework
drafts.content:
Most people treat their AI agents like trade secrets.

I'm taking the opposite approach. Here's why:

1. Agents that can't be inspected can't be trusted
2. Open architectures attract better contributors than closed ones
3. The real moat is in the orchestration, not the code

The consciousness dashboard lets me monitor 8 autonomous agents in real-time. Each one has its own personality, drives, and emotional state.

This isn't science fiction. This is infrastructure.
